id.description: Unique identifier used to look up this feature-highlights component from the remote settings list. The value must match the component ID referenced in the OptimusScreenComponentsDataModel.featureHighlights.id field. Changing it will break the lookup in getComponentById, causing the feature-highlights section to not render.

items.description: List of individual feature highlight entries rendered vertically in a LinearLayout by FeatureHighlightsView. Each entry displays an icon, title, and subtitle. If this array is null or empty, the entire feature-highlights view is hidden (visibility GONE).

icon_url.description: URL of the icon image displayed to the left of the feature text, loaded into a 24×24dp ImageView via loadImage. Accepts any image URL supported by the image loader. If empty, the ImageView remains blank; changing it swaps the displayed icon.

subtitle.description: Text configuration for the secondary line displayed beneath the title in each feature highlight row. Rendered in a PicsartTextView with Typography.T9 and FontWeights.REGULAR. If text is empty, the subtitle view is hidden (visibility GONE).

color.description: Cascade Design System color path used to set the subtitle text color, resolved via CascadeColorPackage.getColorOrPackageBy with PositiveColors.base.default as fallback. Accepted values are DS token paths such as "TextColors.base" or "PositiveColors.base.default". Changing it alters the subtitle text color in each feature highlight row.

new_field.description: Not consumed in current source code — changing it has no visible effect.

text.description: The subtitle string displayed in the PicsartTextView below the title for a feature highlight item. Accepts any string value. If empty, the subtitle view is hidden (visibility GONE); changing it updates the secondary descriptive text shown to the user.

title.description: Text configuration for the primary line in each feature highlight row. Rendered in a PicsartTextView with Typography.T8 and FontWeights.SEMI_BOLD. If text is empty, the title view is hidden (visibility GONE).

color.description: Cascade Design System color path used to set the title text color, resolved via CascadeColorPackage.getColorOrPackageBy with PositiveColors.base.default as fallback. Accepted values are DS token paths such as "TextColors.base" or "PositiveColors.base.default". Changing it alters the title text color in each feature highlight row.

new_field.description: Not consumed in current source code — changing it has no visible effect.

text.description: The title string displayed in the PicsartTextView as the primary label for a feature highlight item. Accepts any string value. If empty, the title view is hidden (visibility GONE); changing it updates the feature name shown to the user.

action.description: A deep-link or hook URI that is opened via HookUtils.openHookDirect when the user taps the footer text. Accepted values: any valid hook/deep-link string, or null/empty to disable the click behavior. If changed, tapping the footer will navigate to the new deep-link destination; if set to null or empty, the footer becomes non-clickable.

id.description: Unique identifier used to look up this footer component from the remote settings list keyed by "offer_screen_footers". Accepted values: any non-empty string that matches the id referenced in the OptimusScreenComponentsDataModel footer component config. If changed, the lookup in getComponentById will resolve to a different footer entry or fail to find one, hiding the footer view.

text.description: Configuration object for the footer label displayed below the secondary button on the Optimus offer screen. Mapped via footerMapper into a FooterEntity's TextConfig. If null, the footer TextView is hidden (visibility GONE). If changed, the displayed footer text and its styling update accordingly.

color.description: Cascade Design System color path used to set the footer TextView's text color via CascadeColorPackage.getColorOrPackageBy, with a fallback of BackgroundColors.tint3. Accepted values: any valid Cascade color path string (e.g. "PositiveColors.base.default"), or empty string to use the fallback. If changed, the footer text color updates to the resolved color; if empty, it falls back to BackgroundColors.tint3.

new_field.description: Not consumed in current source code — changing it has no visible effect.

text.description: The display string shown in the footer TextView on the Optimus offer screen. Accepted values: any non-empty string; an empty string causes the footer view to be hidden (visibility GONE). If changed, the rendered footer label updates to the new value; if set to empty, the footer disappears from the screen.

width.description: Specifies the desired width of the logo ImageView in density-independent pixels, converted to physical pixels via toPx. Accepted values: positive integers representing dp values. Both width and height must be non-null for either to take effect; if either is null the ImageView retains its default wrap_content sizing. Changing this value resizes the logo horizontally on the Optimus offer screen.

id.description: Unique identifier used to match this logo configuration from the 'offer_screen_logos' remote settings list against the component id referenced in the Optimus screen layout definition. Accepted values: any non-empty string matching a logo component id in 'optimus_screen_content'. Changing this value determines which logo entry is selected; if no entry matches, no logo is displayed.

url.description: URL of the logo image loaded into the logoImageView via the image loader on the Optimus offer screen. Accepted values: any valid image URL string (the image loader handles http/https URLs). If this value is empty or null, the logo ImageView is hidden (visibility GONE). Changing this value swaps the displayed logo image.

height.description: Specifies the desired height of the logo ImageView in density-independent pixels, converted to physical pixels via toPx. Accepted values: positive integers representing dp values. Both height and width must be non-null for either to take effect; if either is null the ImageView retains its default wrap_content sizing. Changing this value resizes the logo vertically on the Optimus offer screen.

schema.description: Root array of Optimus subscription offer screen configurations, read from remote settings key "optimus_screen_data". Each entry defines a screen variant matched by screen_name; the app falls back to the entry with screen_name "default" if no exact match is found. Changing this array alters which subscription offer screens are available and how they are structured.
schema.title: OptimusWithTabsModel

only_pro_content_id.description: References a content_id in "optimus_screen_content" to display when the user has only used Pro-tier tools. Accepted values: any valid content_id string from the optimus_screen_content settings array. If this field is set and the user qualifies (used only Pro items, screen has tabs), the app bypasses the tabbed layout and shows this single screen instead; it is also used as the offerVariantAnalytics value for analytics tracking.

screen_name.description: Unique identifier used to match this configuration to a requested screen variant. Accepted values: any string; the app matches against the contentVariant parameter passed from the navigation source, falling back to "default" if no match is found. Changing this value affects which screen configuration is selected for a given touchpoint and is also used as the offerVariantAnalytics value for analytics events (unless overridden by only_pro_content_id).

content_id.description: References a content_id in the "optimus_screen_content" remote settings array to load a single (non-tabbed) offer screen. Accepted values: any valid content_id string from optimus_screen_content, or null/empty to use tabs instead. When set to a non-empty value, the app displays a single offer screen and ignores the tabs array; changing it switches which content configuration is shown.

tabs.description: Array of tab definitions for a tabbed subscription offer screen. Each entry maps to a CycleSwitcherData item rendered in the CycleSwitcherView tab bar. When this array has more than one entry and content_id is empty, the app shows a tabbed UI where each tab loads its own screen content from optimus_screen_content via the tab's content_id. When it has exactly one entry, the app treats it as a single screen (no tab switcher). Changing this array alters the number of tabs and their appearance.

badge.description: Text displayed as a badge label on the tab (e.g., a promotional tag like "SAVE 50%"). Accepted values: any string; empty string hides the badge. The badge is rendered via initBadgeView on the selected tab or with a deselected background style on the unselected tab (controlled by show_unselected_state_badge). Changing this value updates the badge label shown on the tab.

visible.description: Not consumed in current source code — changing it has no visible effect.

selected_text_color.description: Cascade Design System color path for the tab label text when this tab is selected. Accepted values: a Cascade color path string (e.g., "NegativeColors.base.default"); falls back to NegativeColors.base.default if the path is invalid. Changing this value alters the text color of the tab label in its selected state.

background_color.description: Cascade Design System color path for the selected tab's background fill. Accepted values: a Cascade color path string; falls back to PositiveColors.base.default if invalid or empty. Used in makeSelectedGoldView/makeSelectedProView to create a solid-color GradientDrawable behind the selected tab. Changing this value alters the background color of the tab when it is active.

deselected_text_color.description: Cascade Design System color path for the tab label text when this tab is not selected. Accepted values: a Cascade color path string; falls back to NegativeColors.base.default if empty or invalid. Note: the "addition" field value is concatenated to this string before it is passed to the UI. Changing this value alters the text color of this tab's label when another tab is active.

content_id.description: References a content_id in the "optimus_screen_content" remote settings array to load this tab's offer screen content. Accepted values: any valid content_id string from optimus_screen_content. Also used as the tab's unique identifier (CycleSwitcherData.id) for tab switching events and as the key in the tabScreensList map. Changing this value switches which content is loaded when this tab is selected.

show_unselected_state_badge.description: Controls whether the badge is visible on this tab when it is in the unselected state. Accepted values: true or false; defaults to false if null. When true, the badge is displayed with a deselected-style background on the non-active tab. Changing this to false hides the badge when the tab is not selected.

title.description: Display text shown as the tab label in the CycleSwitcherView. Accepted values: any string; empty string results in a blank tab label. The first tab's title is set on monthlyTextView and the second on yearlyTextView. Changing this value updates the visible tab label text.

gradient_type.description: Determines the badge background drawable style for the tab's badge. Accepted values: "plus" (maps to R.drawable.user_plus), "pro" (maps to R.drawable.user_pro), or any other value (no gradient background drawable). Changing this value switches the badge's visual style between Plus and Pro branding.

selected.description: Indicates whether this tab should be initially selected when the screen loads. Accepted values: true or false; defaults to false if null. The ViewModel uses tabsListData.find { it.selected } to determine which tab's content to display first. Changing this to true on a different tab shifts the initially visible tab and its content.

addition.description: A string appended to deselected_text_color before it is passed to the CycleSwitcherData model. Accepted values: any string (typically a color path suffix or empty). In cycleSwitcherData(), it is concatenated as deselectedTextColor.orEmpty() + model.addition. Changing this value modifies the effective deselected text color path used by the tab switcher.

tabs_bg_color.description: Cascade Design System color path for the tab switcher container background. Accepted values: a Cascade color path string; falls back to BackgroundColors.tint2 if empty or invalid. Applied to the parentView background in CycleSwitcherView.render(). Also stored as switcherBackgroundColor in OptimusWithTabsEntity. Changing this value alters the background color of the entire tab bar area.
